generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuators for HVAC, lighting, and energy management systems in smart buildings, controlled via BIM-enriched models"
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems that adjust temperature, airflow, and humidity in real-time"
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators controlling building lighting systems based on occupancy, daylight levels, and energy optimization requirements"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators implementing energy-saving strategies through dynamic control of HVAC, lighting, and building envelope components"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature sensors distributed throughout building zones for continuous monitoring of HVAC system performance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensors measuring indoor air quality and environmental conditions for HVAC system optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityData
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion and presence sensors detecting building occupancy levels for dynamic lighting and HVAC control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyData
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption meters tracking HVAC, lighting, and building envelope energy usage patterns"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionData
]

instance AnomalyDetectionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensors detecting operational anomalies (sensor drift, actuator failures) via automated alert generation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AnomalyDetectionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance AnomalyDetectionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AnomalyDetectionSensor
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Synchronization metrics and data streams for real-time state alignment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData, HumidityData, OccupancyData, EnergyConsumptionData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM models incorporating spatial, structural, and physical attributes of building components with embedded BCS metadata"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance BehavioralControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic models defined by Building Control Engineers encapsulating device behavior for HVAC, lighting, and energy systems"
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured representations generated via MDE for continuous synchronization between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance OperationalAnomalyDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical models for detecting operational anomalies (sensor drift, actuator failures) via automated alert generation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataValidationFramework
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics model for forecasting equipment failures and maintenance needs based on historical data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomAnalyticsServices
]

instance EnergyOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimization model for determining energy-efficient HVAC and lighting configurations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomAnalyticsServices
]

instance DataConsistencyMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metrics tracking data consistency between physical and digital twins (drift thresholds, latency benchmarks)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeSynchronizationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataValidationFramework
]

instance OperationalAnomalyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Detected anomalies (sensor drift, actuator failures) generated via automated alerts from real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AnomalyDetectionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AlertNotificationSystem
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsights : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance recommendations based on equipment health metrics and historical failure patterns"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionData, OccupancyData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MaintenanceScheduler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE workflows automating extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models for DT artifacts"
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange protocol enabling bidirectional synchronization between physical building components and their digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSynchronizationData
]

instance Ditto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Protocol facilitating continuous data exchange and model synchronization between physical and digital building systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSynchronizationData
]

instance DataValidationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework ensuring data consistency metrics and validation between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataConsistencyMetricsData
]

instance AlertNotificationSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System generating automated alerts for operational anomalies detected in real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalAnomalyData
]

instance MaintenanceScheduler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System scheduling maintenance activities based on predictive maintenance insights"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceInsights
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Building Control Engineers to define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within BIM environments"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateMonitoring, OperationalAlerts
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical buildings and their digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateMonitoring, OperationalAlerts
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling DT Engineers to deploy custom analytics, automation, and plugin services via modular architecture"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveAnalytics, CustomControlLogic
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting energy optimization and predictive maintenance through data-driven insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendations, MaintenanceForecasting
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of physical-DT state alignment enabling real-time operational insights"
]

instance OperationalAlerts : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated alerts for operational anomalies (sensor drift, actuator failures) generated via real-time monitoring"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Context-aware recommendations for optimal energy settings based on real-time building conditions"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceForecasting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Equipment failure predictions and maintenance scheduling based on historical data patterns"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DashboardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard visualizations of system states, operational metrics, and anomalies"
]

instance ImmersiveControl : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive interfaces for facility managers to adjust controls and monitor building operations in real-time"
]

instance PredictiveAnalytics : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Custom analytics implementations (energy forecasting, predictive maintenance) via embedded data processing"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomControlLogic : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "DT Engineers' custom control logic implementations integrated via modular extension points"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ValidationMetrics : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data consistency metrics (drift thresholds, latency benchmarks) validating DT accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyCorrection : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated correction of detected anomalies in real-time monitoring systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlOverrideAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facility Managers' ability to manually override automated decisions (e.g., HVAC settings)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlLogicValidation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Building Control Engineers' validation of control logic before deployment via DT"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance SmartBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HVACActuator, LightingActuator, EnergyOptimizationActuator,
                 TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor,
                 EnergyConsumptionSensor, AnomalyDetectionSensor,
                 BIMEnrichedGeometricModel, BehavioralControlLogicModel,
                 RealTimeSynchronizationModel, OperationalAnomalyDetectionModel,
                 PredictiveMaintenanceModel, EnergyOptimizationModel,
                 EclipseHono, Ditto,
                 DataValidationFramework, AlertNotificationSystem, MaintenanceScheduler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing all building control system components and their digital twins"
    base:contains BuildingControlSystemActuator, SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The framework facilitates bidirectional communication where control signals are fed back to **Building Control Systems (BCS)** to operate the physical infrastructure via the Digital Twin (DT). Facility managers interact with the DT to monitor and control physical assets, with explicit roles ensuring command validation and execution confirmation. While the document does not specify explicit protocols or message formats, the bidirectional nature is emphasized through the DT Framework's central hub role, enabling real-time adjustments and operational oversight.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **real-time synchronization** between cyber-physical assets and their virtual representations, enabling live data exchange and immediate correlation of physical and digital states. This is supported by **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** frameworks, which facilitate low-latency data transmission for real-time monitoring, simulation, and predictive analytics. The system prioritizes **sub-second latency** for critical operations, ensuring timely decision-making across the building lifecycle. While the documents do not specify exact temporal granularity or dynamic scaling for services, the architecture inherently supports **high-frequency monitoring** and **adaptive temporal resolution** to accommodate varying operational demands (e.g., predictive maintenance vs. routine inspections)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation in AECO adopts an **orchestrated, component-integrated architecture** leveraging **cyber-physical integration patterns** to unify static models (e.g., BIM) with runtime data streams. Key coordination mechanisms include **real-time data fusion** (e.g., through IoT/edge computing) and **service-oriented architectures** for modular component deployment. Resource management is implied to be **distributed**, with emphasis on **scalability** to handle heterogeneous infrastructure (e.g., buildings with diverse sensors). The documents suggest a **lifecycle-aware orchestration** where DTs evolve from design-phase simulations to operational analytics, though no specific orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes, custom middleware) or patterns (e.g., microservices) are detailed."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle spans **complete life cycle deployment and operation**, addressing the gap between BIM’s static representations and the dynamic nature of DTs. The framework supports **four core phases**:

1. **Device Modeling and BIM Enrichment**:
   - Building Control Engineers (BCEs) model **BCS (Building Control Systems) components** (sensors, actuators, controllers) directly within BIM environments, overcoming its limitation in representing dynamic BCS elements.
   - Enriched BIM models provide a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations, enabling structured representations via **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles.

2. **Transformation and Artifact Generation**:
   - MDE principles extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, automating the generation of **deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
   - This phase ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations, leveraging **automated transformation workflows**.

3. **Real-Time Synchronization and Operation**:
   - The DT operates in a **continuous synchronization loop** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, ensuring **real-time data exchange** for monitoring and control.
   - **Interactive visualization interfaces** support immersive monitoring and operational control by Facility Managers (FMs).

4. **Custom Service Development and Evolution**:
   - DT Engineers develop and integrate **custom services and plugins** via a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points.
   - The framework supports **runtime evolution** of DTs to adapt to changing operational requirements, enabling iterative enhancements through **custom analytics and plugins**.

Lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by the **stakeholder-centric use cases** (BCEs, FMs, DT Engineers) and the **modular, extensible architecture**, ensuring alignment with operational needs across the DT lifecycle.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **real-time data exchange** to maintain entanglement between physical and digital domains, ensuring dynamic fidelity. **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** is implied as a metric for validating model alignment with real-world behavior.

While explicit validation protocols are not detailed, the framework’s **model-driven architecture** supports implicit quality assurance through:
- **Automated transformation pipelines** from BIM/BCS models to runtime artifacts, reducing manual error introduction.
- **Modular extension points** enable iterative refinement of DT services, aligning with operational changes.
- **Runtime synchronization** ensures data consistency, though specific error detection/correction mechanisms or uncertainty quantification methods are not specified in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The technical network infrastructure employs **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for real-time data exchange, ensuring low-latency synchronization between physical and virtual systems. The modular architecture with standardized extension points supports service-oriented integration, enabling adaptability to evolving operational needs. Non-functional requirements (NF_X) address interoperability across heterogeneous systems, though security protocols and redundancy mechanisms are **not explicitly detailed** in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin Framework operates as a **centralized hub**, hosting the DT lifecycle through a runtime-configured, modular architecture. Deployment is supported via **low-overhead updates** and scalable components, allowing incremental adjustments without full redeployment. The system emphasizes **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, though cloud/edge/on-premise hybrid architectures or explicit reliability assurance mechanisms are **not mentioned**. Accessibility is ensured for users with varying technical expertise, aligning with maintainability and operational flexibility.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** frameworks and leverages **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** for real-time data exchange. While the documents do not explicitly mention interoperability standards like IFC, REST APIs, MQTT, or WebSockets, the emphasis on **data-driven infrastructure management** implies reliance on standardized protocols for seamless interoperability with enterprise systems and other Digital Twins. The integration patterns are designed to support **fragmented model/data dependencies** and enable cross-domain collaboration across the building lifecycle."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework addresses standardization gaps by adopting **BIM as an AECO-specific standard** (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) to ensure semantic consistency across DT components. Standardized extension points enable modular integration of custom services, while the framework promotes **standardized BIM semantics** to mitigate the limitations of BIM’s static representations. The absence of universal integration frameworks is acknowledged as a challenge, but the proposed solution emphasizes **MDE-driven automation** and **modular architecture** to enhance interoperability. Compliance frameworks are not explicitly detailed, but the emphasis on **semantic alignment** and **extension-point standardization** aligns with industry efforts to improve DT interoperability.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly address cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, authentication systems, fail-safe mechanisms, threat mitigation strategies, or safety-critical system considerations for Digital Twin (DT) systems in the AECO domain. While the context highlights challenges related to **semantic drift**—where misalignment between digital and physical models occurs over time—this pertains to model integrity rather than security or safety frameworks. The documents emphasize **model-driven engineering (MDE)** for governance via organizational policies and architectural decision encoding, but no specific cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption standards, intrusion detection systems) or safety-critical mechanisms (e.g., fail-safes for critical DT operations) are mentioned. Thus, these aspects remain undefined in the provided literature."
]
